summary: hardwareLockV2 local forensic sweep exhausted the current box as a recovery source; confirmed empty runtime placeholders, explicit external-repair dependency, and a strong historical setup.enc provisioning lead from shell history.
key_points: Recovered a historical provisioning command that fetched setup.enc, decrypted it with passphrase theNTVofthe360isthe360oftheNTV, and piped it to sudo bash.
Confirmed /opt/nctv-player, /var/lib/nctv-player, and /mnt/nctv-phoenix-secure are empty placeholders while repairman.sh requires an external nctv-phoenix tree for surgical restore.
Updated hardwareLockV2 exploit docs, evidence register, findings register, attack paths, and master activity log with EVI-008 through EVI-011.
next_actions: When resuming hardwareLockV2, pivot to artifact acquisition: external repair tree, sibling device, or historical setup.enc installer artifact.
Avoid more blind on-box unlock attempts or service edits; the current device is locally exhausted as a recovery source.
